title: Isolepis humillima
intro: Isolepis humillima, also known as the dwarf club rush, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Isolepis humillima include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Isolepis humillima include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Isolepis humillima belongs to the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. This classification is based on its characteristics and similarities with other plants in the Cyperaceae family. The genus Isolepis is a group of sedge plants that are typically found in wet environments. Isolepis humillima is a specific species within this genus, sharing many traits with other Isolepis species. Its classification as a member of the Cyperaceae family helps in understanding its evolutionary relationships and characteristics.
